resolvedQuestions: Q1: use Node CJS persist-artifacts helper with parser exports, fixture tests, strict-required sections, optional-section degradation, and exit codes 0/1/2
Q2: add short normative §17 to the agent body; keep long examples and schema details in references
Q3: create markdown output-schema.md as the shared §8 contract and parser requiredness source
Q4: keep validate.sh --strict free-form for ai-council/; add only opt-in council-aware advisory checks after helper exists
Q5: do not add Skill Advisor boosts for multi-ai-council while it remains an agent rather than a skill
Q6: add normalized four-runtime mirror parity at test time; avoid byte-for-byte equality
Q7: preserve bare event rows and add only optional schema_version/protocol/producer metadata for v1.1
Q8: do not create council-specific /memory:save anchors; optionally emit routed save payloads through existing categories
Q9: mitigate ADD-1..ADD-6 with caller-owned helper invocation, one schema source, forward-only scope, and helper-first sequencing
Q10: revisit ADR-001 only if council needs skill-like lifecycle ownership, reducer state, automatic routing, enforced validation, generated mirrors, or helper-controlled execution
keyFindings: Packet 081 should ship helper script, §17 caller protocol, output-schema.md, vitest fixtures, validator hardening, and mirror parity.
Packet 082+ should defer state v1.1 polish, advisor regression guard, optional /memory:save payloads, council-aware advisory checks, and command YAML wiring.
Current packet 080 integration is solid for a lightweight convention but incomplete operationally until persistence and schema tooling land.
The lightweight bound remains valid; no dedicated multi-ai-council skill folder is justified by the researched follow-ons.
ruledOutDirections: JSON Schema as the primary §8 contract
Required type discriminator or migration of packet 080 state rows
Retroactive migration of pre-080 council outputs
